1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: module RubyLLM
4:   # Base class for wire protocols. A protocol knows how to talk to a family
5:   # of APIs: rendering payloads, parsing responses, streaming chunks, and the
6:   # endpoints involved. Providers know where to talk and who they are.
7:   class Protocol
8:     include Streaming
9: 
10:     attr_reader :provider, :config, :connection, :model
11: 
12:     def initialize(provider, model = nil)
13:       @provider = provider
14:       @config = provider.config
15:       @connection = provider.connection
16:       @model = model
17:     end
18: 
19:     # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
20:     def complete(messages, tools:, temperature:, params: {}, headers: {}, schema: nil, thinking: nil,
21:                  citations: false, tool_prefs: nil, &)
22:       normalized_temperature = maybe_normalize_temperature(temperature, model)
23: 
24:       payload = Utils.deep_merge(
25:         render_payload(
26:           messages,
27:           tools: tools,
28:           tool_prefs: tool_prefs,
29:           temperature: normalized_temperature,
30:           model: model,
31:           stream: block_given?,
32:           schema: schema,
33:           thinking: thinking,
34:           citations: citations
35:         ),
36:         params
37:       )
38: 
39:       if block_given?
40:         stream_response payload, headers, &
41:       else
42:         sync_response payload, headers
43:       end
44:     end
45:     # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
46: 
47:     def list_models
48:       response = @connection.get models_url
49:       parse_list_models_response response, @provider.slug, @provider.capabilities
50:     end
51: 
52:     def embed(text, model:, dimensions:)
53:       payload = render_embedding_payload(text, model:, dimensions:)
54:       response = @connection.post(embedding_url(model:), payload)
55:       parse_embedding_response(response, model:, text:)
56:     end
57: 
58:     def moderate(input, model:)
59:       payload = render_moderation_payload(input, model:)
60:       response = @connection.post moderation_url, payload
61:       parse_moderation_response(response, model:)
62:     end
63: 
64:     def paint(prompt, model:, size:, with: nil, mask: nil, params: {}) # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
65:       validate_paint_inputs!(with:, mask:)
66:       payload = render_image_payload(prompt, model:, size:, with:, mask:, params:)
67:       response = @connection.post images_url(with:, mask:), payload
68:       parse_image_response(response, model:)
69:     end
70: 
71:     def transcribe(audio_file, model:, language:, **options)
72:       file_part = build_audio_file_part(audio_file)
73:       payload = render_transcription_payload(file_part, model:, language:, **options)
74:       response = @connection.post transcription_url, payload
75:       parse_transcription_response(response, model:)
76:     end
77: 
78:     def maybe_normalize_temperature(temperature, _model)
79:       temperature
80:     end
81: 
82:     def parse_error(response)
83:       @provider.parse_error(response)
84:     end
85: 
86:     private
87: 
88:     def validate_paint_inputs!(with:, mask:)
89:       return if with.nil? && mask.nil?
90: 
91:       raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, 'image reference'
92:     end
93: 
94:     def build_audio_file_part(file_path)
95:       require 'faraday/multipart'
96:       require 'marcel'
97:       require 'pathname'
98: 
99:       expanded_path = File.expand_path(file_path)
100:       mime_type = Marcel::MimeType.for(Pathname.new(expanded_path))
101: 
102:       Faraday::Multipart::FilePart.new(
103:         expanded_path,
104:         mime_type,
105:         File.basename(expanded_path)
106:       )
107:     end
108: 
109:     def sync_response(payload, additional_headers = {})
110:       response = @connection.post completion_url, payload do |req|
111:         req.headers = additional_headers.merge(req.headers) unless additional_headers.empty?
112:       end
113:       parse_completion_response response
114:     end
115:   end

5: module RubyLLM
6:   # Global configuration for RubyLLM
7:   class Configuration
8:     class << self
9:       # Declare a single configuration option.
10:       def option(key, default = nil)
11:         key = key.to_sym
12:         return if options.include?(key)
13: 
14:         attr_reader key
15: 
16:         define_method("#{key}=") do |value|
17:           value = nil if value.is_a?(String) && value.strip.empty?
18:           instance_variable_set(:"@#{key}", value)
19:         end
20: 
21:         option_keys << key
22:         defaults[key] = default
23:       end
24: 
25:       def register_provider_options(options)
26:         Array(options).each { |key| option(key, nil) }
27:       end
28: 
29:       def options
30:         option_keys.dup
31:       end
32: 
33:       private

36:       def defaults = @defaults ||= {}
37:       private :option

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   # Custom error class that wraps API errors from different providers
5:   # into a consistent format with helpful error messages.
6:   class Error < StandardError
7:     attr_reader :response
8: 
9:     def self.default_message
10:       nil
11:     end
12: 
13:     def initialize(response = nil, message = nil)
14:       if response.is_a?(String)
15:         message = response
16:         response = nil
17:       end
18: 
19:       @response = response
20:       super(message || response&.body || self.class.default_message)
21:     end
22:   end
23: 
24:   # Error classes for non-HTTP errors
25:   class ConfigurationError < StandardError; end
26:   class PromptNotFoundError < StandardError; end
27:   class InvalidRoleError < StandardError; end
28:   class InvalidToolChoiceError < StandardError; end
29:   class ModelNotFoundError < StandardError; end
30: 
31:   # Raised when RubyLLM cannot format an attachment for the selected provider.
32:   class UnsupportedAttachmentError < StandardError
33:     GUIDANCE = 'Consider using a model that supports this attachment type.'
34: 
35:     def initialize(type = nil)
36:       message = 'Unsupported attachment type'
37:       message = "#{message}: #{type}" if type
38:       super("#{message}. #{GUIDANCE}")
39:     end
40:   end
41: 
42:   # Error classes for different HTTP status codes
43:   # Raised when the API request is invalid.
44:   class BadRequestError < Error
45:     def self.default_message
46:       'Invalid request - please check your input'
47:     end
48:   end
49: 
50:   # Raised when the API key or account lacks permission for the request.
51:   class ForbiddenError < Error
52:     def self.default_message
53:       'Forbidden - you do not have permission to access this resource'
54:     end
55:   end
56: 
57:   # Raised when token or context limits are exceeded for a request.
58:   class ContextLengthExceededError < Error
59:     def self.default_message
60:       'Context length exceeded'
61:     end
62:   end
63: 
64:   # Raised when the service signals temporary overload.
65:   class OverloadedError < Error
66:     def self.default_message
67:       'Service overloaded - please try again later'
68:     end
69:   end
70: 
71:   # Raised when account billing prevents the request from being completed.
72:   class PaymentRequiredError < Error
73:     def self.default_message
74:       'Payment required - please top up your account'
75:     end
76:   end
77: 
78:   # Raised when rate limits are exceeded.
79:   class RateLimitError < Error
80:     def self.default_message
81:       'Rate limit exceeded - please wait a moment'
82:     end
83:   end
84: 
85:   # Raised when the provider returns a server-side error.
86:   class ServerError < Error
87:     def self.default_message
88:       'API server error - please try again'
89:     end
90:   end
91: 
92:   # Raised when the provider is temporarily unavailable.
93:   class ServiceUnavailableError < Error
94:     def self.default_message
95:       'API server unavailable - please try again later'
96:     end
97:   end
98: 
99:   # Raised when authentication fails.
100:   class UnauthorizedError < Error
101:     def self.default_message
102:       'Invalid API key - check your credentials'
103:     end
104:   end
105: end

132:       def resolve(model_id, provider: nil, assume_exists: false, config: nil) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
133:         config ||= RubyLLM.config
134:         provider_class = provider ? Provider.providers[provider.to_sym] : nil
135:         assume_exists = true if provider_class&.local? || provider_class&.assume_models_exist?
136: 
137:         if assume_exists
138:           raise ArgumentError, 'Provider must be specified if assume_exists is true' unless provider
139: 
140:           provider_class ||= raise_unknown_provider(provider)
141:

143:                     begin
144:                       Models.find(model_id, provider)
145:                     rescue ModelNotFoundError
146:                       nil
147:                     end
148:                   end
149: 
150:           model ||= Model::Info.default(model_id, provider_class.slug)
151:         else
152:           model = Models.find model_id, provider
153:           provider_class = Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym] || raise_unknown_provider(model.provider)
154:         end

174:           end
175:         end

177:       rescue StandardError => e

236:           models_dev_model = find_models_dev_model(key, models_dev_by_key)

239:           if models_dev_model && provider_model

241:           elsif models_dev_model
242:             models_dev_model
243:           else
244:             provider_model

257:       def find_models_dev_model(key, models_dev_by_key)
258:         # Direct match
259:         return models_dev_by_key[key] if models_dev_by_key[key]
260: 
261:         provider, model_id = key.split(':', 2)
262:         if provider == 'bedrock'
263:           normalized_id = model_id.sub(/^[a-z]{2}\./, '')
264:           context_override = nil
265:           normalized_id = normalized_id.gsub(/:(\d+)k\b/) do
266:             context_override = Regexp.last_match(1).to_i * 1000
267:             ''
268:           end
269:           bedrock_model = models_dev_by_key["bedrock:#{normalized_id}"]
270:           if bedrock_model
271:             data = bedrock_model.to_h.merge(id: model_id)
272:             data[:context_window] = context_override if context_override
273:             return Model::Info.new(data)
274:           end
275:         end
276: 
277:         # VertexAI uses same models as Gemini
278:         return unless provider == 'vertexai'
279: 
280:         gemini_model = models_dev_by_key["gemini:#{model_id}"]
281:         return unless gemini_model

283:         # Return Gemini's models.dev data but with VertexAI as provider
284:         Model::Info.new(gemini_model.to_h.merge(provider: 'vertexai'))

444:     end

452:     end

455:       if provider
456:         find_with_provider(model_id, provider)
457:       else
458:         find_without_provider(model_id)
459:       end
460:     end

464:     end

468:     end

472:     end

476:     end

480:     end

484:     end

487:       @models = self.class.fetch_merged_models(remote_only: remote_only)
488:       self
489:     end

491:     def resolve(model_id, provider: nil, assume_exists: false, config: nil)
492:       self.class.resolve(model_id, provider: provider, assume_exists: assume_exists, config: config)
493:     end
494: 
495:     private
496: 
497:     def find_with_provider(model_id, provider)
498:       resolved_id = Aliases.resolve(model_id, provider)
499:       resolved_id = resolve_bedrock_region_id(resolved_id) if provider.to_s == 'bedrock'
500:       all.find { |m| m.id == resolved_id && m.provider == provider.to_s } ||
501:         all.find { |m| m.id == model_id && m.provider == provider.to_s } ||
502:         raise_model_not_found(model_id, provider: provider)
503:     end
504: 
505:     def resolve_bedrock_region_id(model_id)
506:       region = RubyLLM.config.bedrock_region.to_s
507:       return model_id if region.empty?
508: 
509:       candidate_id = Providers::Bedrock::Models.with_region_prefix(model_id, region)
510:       return model_id if candidate_id == model_id
511: 
512:       candidate = all.find { |m| m.provider == 'bedrock' && m.id == candidate_id }
513:       return model_id unless candidate
514: 
515:       inference_types = Array(candidate.metadata[:inference_types] || candidate.metadata['inference_types'])
516:       Providers::Bedrock::Models.normalize_inference_profile_id(model_id, inference_types, region)
517:     end
518: 
519:     def find_without_provider(model_id)
520:       exact_matches = all.select { |m| m.id == model_id }
521:       return preferred_match(exact_matches) if exact_matches.any?
522: 
523:       resolved_id = Aliases.resolve(model_id)
524:       alias_matches = all.select { |m| m.id == resolved_id }
525:       return preferred_match(alias_matches) if alias_matches.any?
526: 
527:       raise_model_not_found(model_id)
528:     end
529: 
530:     def raise_model_not_found(model_id, provider: nil)
531:       message = "Unknown model: #{model_id.inspect}"
532:       message = "#{message} for provider: #{provider.inspect}" if provider
533: 
534:       raise ModelNotFoundError, "#{message}. #{refresh_registry_guidance}"
535:     end
536: 
537:     def refresh_registry_guidance
538:       rails_model = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_class
539:       'If the model exists at the provider, refresh the registry with `RubyLLM.models.refresh!` ' \
540:         'and persist it with `RubyLLM.models.save_to_json`. ' \
541:         "Rails model registries can call `#{rails_model}.refresh!` instead."
542:     end
543: 
544:     def preferred_match(candidates)
545:       return candidates.first if candidates.size == 1
546: 
547:       candidates.min_by do |model|
548:         index = PROVIDER_PREFERENCE.index(model.provider)
549:         index || PROVIDER_PREFERENCE.length
550:       end
551:     end
552:   end
553: end

9: module RubyLLM
10:   # Connection class for managing API connections to various providers.
11:   class Connection
12:     attr_reader :provider, :connection, :config
13: 
14:     def self.basic(&)
15:       Faraday.new do |f|
16:         f.response :logger,
17:                    RubyLLM.logger,
18:                    bodies: false,
19:                    errors: true,
20:                    headers: false,
21:                    log_level: :debug
22:         f.response :raise_error
23:         yield f if block_given?
24:       end
25:     end
26: 
27:     def initialize(provider, config)
28:       @provider = provider
29:       @config = config
30: 
31:       @connection = Faraday.new(provider.api_base) do |faraday|
32:         setup_timeout(faraday)
33:         setup_logging(faraday)
34:         setup_retry(faraday)
35:         setup_middleware(faraday)
36:         setup_http_proxy(faraday)
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     def post(url, payload, &)
41:       instrument_request(:post, url) do
42:         @connection.post url, payload do |req|
43:           req.headers.merge! @provider.headers
44:           yield req if block_given?
45:         end
46:       end
47:     end
48: 
49:     def get(url, &)
50:       instrument_request(:get, url) do
51:         @connection.get url do |req|
52:           req.headers.merge! @provider.headers
53:           yield req if block_given?
54:         end
55:       end
56:     end
57: 
58:     # Keeps the config and Faraday internals out of pretty-printed output.
59:     def pretty_print_instance_variables
60:       super - %i[@config @connection]
61:     end
62: 
63:     private
64: 
65:     def instrument_request(method, url)
66:       payload = {
67:         provider: @provider.slug,
68:         method: method,
69:         url: url
70:       }
71: 
72:       RubyLLM.instrument('request.ruby_llm', payload, config: @config) do
73:         response = yield
74:         payload[:status] = response.status if response.respond_to?(:status)
75:         response
76:       end
77:     end
78: 
79:     def setup_timeout(faraday)
80:       faraday.options.timeout = @config.request_timeout
81:     end
82: 
83:     def setup_logging(faraday)
84:       faraday.response :logger,
85:                        RubyLLM.logger,
86:                        bodies: RubyLLM.logger.debug?,
87:                        errors: true,
88:                        headers: false,
89:                        log_level: :debug do |logger|
90:         logger.filter(logging_regexp('[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{100,}'), '[BASE64 DATA]')
91:         logger.filter(logging_regexp('[-\\d.e,\\s]{100,}'), '[EMBEDDINGS ARRAY]')
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     def logging_regexp(pattern)
96:       return Regexp.new(pattern) if @config.log_regexp_timeout.nil? || !Regexp.respond_to?(:timeout)
97: 
98:       Regexp.new(pattern, timeout: @config.log_regexp_timeout)
99:     end
100: 
101:     def setup_retry(faraday)
102:       faraday.request :retry, {
103:         max: @config.max_retries,
104:         interval: @config.retry_interval,
105:         interval_randomness: @config.retry_interval_randomness,
106:         backoff_factor: @config.retry_backoff_factor,
107:         methods: Faraday::Retry::Middleware::IDEMPOTENT_METHODS + [:post],
108:         exceptions: retry_exceptions
109:       }
110:     end
111: 
112:     def setup_middleware(faraday)
113:       faraday.request :multipart
114:       faraday.request :json
115:       faraday.response :json
116:       faraday.adapter(@config.faraday_adapter)
117:       faraday.use :llm_errors, provider: @provider
118:     end
119: 
120:     def setup_http_proxy(faraday)
121:       return unless @config.http_proxy
122: 
123:       faraday.proxy = @config.http_proxy
124:     end
125: 
126:     def retry_exceptions
127:       [
128:         Errno::ETIMEDOUT,
129:         Timeout::Error,
130:         Faraday::TimeoutError,
131:         Faraday::ConnectionFailed,
132:         Faraday::RetriableResponse,
133:         RubyLLM::RateLimitError,
134:         RubyLLM::ServerError,
135:         RubyLLM::ServiceUnavailableError,
136:         RubyLLM::OverloadedError
137:       ]
138:     end
139:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'faraday'
4: require 'ruby_llm/error'
5: 
6: module RubyLLM
7:   # Faraday middleware that maps provider-specific API errors to RubyLLM errors.
8:   class ErrorMiddleware < Faraday::Middleware
9:     def initialize(app, options = {})
10:       super(app)
11:       @provider = options[:provider]
12:     end
13: 
14:     def call(env)
15:       @app.call(env).on_complete do |response|
16:         self.class.parse_error(provider: @provider, response: response)
17:       end
18:     end
19: 
20:     class << self
21:       CONTEXT_LENGTH_PATTERNS = [
22:         /context length/i,
23:         /context window/i,
24:         /maximum context/i,
25:         /request too large/i,
26:         /too many tokens/i,
27:         /token count exceeds/i,
28:         /input[_\s-]?token/i,
29:         /input or output tokens? must be reduced/i,
30:         /reduce the length of messages/i,
31:         /prompt is too long/i
32:       ].freeze
33: 
34:       RATE_LIMIT_PATTERNS = [
35:         /rate limit/i,
36:         /per minute/i,
37:         /per hour/i,
38:         /per day/i
39:       ].freeze
40: 
41:       def parse_error(provider:, response:)
42:         message = provider&.parse_error(response)
43: 
44:         case response.status
45:         when 200..399
46:           message
47:         when 400
48:           raise ContextLengthExceededError.new(response, message) if context_length_exceeded?(message)
49: 
50:           raise BadRequestError.new(response, message)
51:         when 401
52:           raise UnauthorizedError.new(response, message)
53:         when 402
54:           raise PaymentRequiredError.new(response, message)
55:         when 403
56:           raise ForbiddenError.new(response, message)
57:         when 429
58:           raise RateLimitError.new(response, message) if rate_limited?(message)
59:           raise ContextLengthExceededError.new(response, message) if context_length_exceeded?(message)
60: 
61:           raise RateLimitError.new(response, message)
62:         when 500
63:           raise ServerError.new(response, message)
64:         when 502..504
65:           raise ServiceUnavailableError.new(response, message)
66:         when 529
67:           raise OverloadedError.new(response, message)
68:         else
69:           raise Error.new(response, message)
70:         end
71:       end
72: 
73:       private
74: 
75:       def context_length_exceeded?(message)
76:         return false if message.to_s.empty?
77: 
78:         CONTEXT_LENGTH_PATTERNS.any? { |pattern| message.match?(pattern) }
79:       end
80: 
81:       def rate_limited?(message)
82:         return false if message.to_s.empty?
83: 
84:         RATE_LIMIT_PATTERNS.any? { |pattern| message.match?(pattern) }
85:       end
86:     end
87:   end
88: end
89: 
90: Faraday::Middleware.register_middleware(llm_errors: RubyLLM::ErrorMiddleware)

5: module RubyLLM

11: 
12:     def initialize(model: nil, provider: nil, assume_model_exists: false, context: nil)
13:       if assume_model_exists && !provider
14:         raise ArgumentError, 'Provider must be specified if assume_model_exists is true'
15:       end

17:       @context = context

42:       add_message role: :user, content: build_content(message, with)

44:     end

48:     def with_instructions(instructions, append: false, replace: nil)
49:       unless replace.nil?

51:           '`replace:` is deprecated and will be removed in RubyLLM 2.0. ' \
52:           '`with_instructions` replaces by default; use `append: true` to append.'
53:         )

57:       if append
58:         append_system_instruction(instructions)

62: 
63:       self
64:     end

67:       unless tool.nil?
68:         tool_instance = tool.is_a?(Class) ? tool.new : tool
69:         @tools[tool_instance.name.to_sym] = tool_instance

84:     def with_model(model_id, provider: nil, assume_exists: false)

93:     end

100:     end

105:     end

107:     def with_context(context)

112:     end

116:       self
117:     end

173:     end

177:     end

179:     def complete(&block)
180:       result = nil
181:       payload = {
182:         chat: self,
183:         provider: @provider.slug,
184:         provider_class: @provider.class.name,
185:         model: @model.id,
186:         model_info: @model,
187:         input_messages: messages.dup,
188:         message_count: messages.size,
189:         tools: tools.keys,
190:         tool_choice: tool_prefs[:choice],
191:         tool_call_limit: tool_prefs[:calls],
192:         temperature: @temperature,
193:         params: params,
194:         schema: schema,
195:         thinking: @thinking,
196:         citations: @citations,
197:         streaming: block_given?
198:       }
199: 
200:       RubyLLM.instrument('chat.ruby_llm', payload, config: @config) do |event|
201:         result = complete_once(&block)
202:         event[:response] = result
203:         event[:messages_after] = messages.dup
204:         event[:response_role] = result.role if result.respond_to?(:role)
205: 
206:         if result.respond_to?(:tool_call?)
207:           event[:response_model] = result.model_id
208:           event[:tool_call] = result.tool_call?
209:           event[:tool_calls] = result.tool_calls
210:           event[:input_tokens] = result.input_tokens
211:           event[:output_tokens] = result.output_tokens
212:           event[:cached_tokens] = result.cached_tokens
213:           event[:cache_creation_tokens] = result.cache_creation_tokens
214:           event[:thinking_tokens] = result.thinking_tokens
215:         end
216:       end
217:       result
218:     end
219: 
220:     def add_message(message_or_attributes)
221:       message = message_or_attributes.is_a?(Message) ? message_or_attributes : Message.new(message_or_attributes)
222:       messages << message
223:       message
224:     end
225: 
226:     # Mutates this chat by removing all in-memory messages.
227:     def reset_messages!
228:       @messages.clear
229:     end
230: 
231:     # Keeps the connection and config dumps out of pretty-printed output.
232:     def pretty_print_instance_variables
233:       super - %i[@connection @config]
234:     end
235: 
236:     private
237: 
238:     def normalize_schema_payload(raw_schema)
239:       return nil if raw_schema.nil?
240:       return raw_schema unless raw_schema.is_a?(Hash)
241: 
242:       schema = RubyLLM::Utils.deep_symbolize_keys(raw_schema)
243:       schema_def = extract_schema_definition(schema)
244:       strict = extract_schema_strict(schema, schema_def)
245:       build_schema_payload(schema, schema_def, strict)
246:     end
247: 
248:     def extract_schema_definition(schema)
249:       RubyLLM::Utils.deep_dup(schema[:schema] || schema)
250:     end
251: 
252:     def extract_schema_strict(schema, schema_def)
253:       return schema[:strict] if schema.key?(:strict)
254:       return schema_def.delete(:strict) if schema_def.is_a?(Hash)
255: 
256:       nil
257:     end
258: 
259:     def build_schema_payload(schema, schema_def, strict)
260:       {
261:         name: sanitize_schema_name(schema[:name] || 'response'),
262:         schema: schema_def,
263:         strict: strict.nil? || strict,
264:         description: schema[:description]
265:       }.compact
266:     end
267: 
268:     def sanitize_schema_name(name)
269:       sanitized = name.to_s.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/, '_')
270:       sanitized.empty? ? 'response' : sanitized
271:     end
272: 
273:     def add_callback(name, &block)
274:       @callbacks[name] << block if block
275:       self
276:     end
277: 
278:     def complete_once(&)
279:       response = provider_completion(&)
280: 
281:       run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message) unless block_given?
282: 
283:       normalize_schema_response(response)
284: 
285:       add_message response
286:       run_callbacks(:after_message, :end_message, response)
287: 
288:       if response.tool_call?
289:         handle_tool_calls(response, &)
290:       else
291:         response
292:       end
293:     end
294: 
295:     def provider_completion(&)
296:       @provider.complete(
297:         messages,
298:         tools: @tools,
299:         tool_prefs: @tool_prefs,
300:         temperature: @temperature,
301:         model: @model,
302:         params: @params,
303:         headers: @headers,
304:         schema: @schema,
305:         thinking: @thinking,
306:         citations: @citations,
307:         protocol: @protocol,
308:         &wrap_streaming_block(&)
309:       )
310:     end
311: 
312:     def normalize_schema_response(response)
313:       return unless @schema && response.content.is_a?(String) && !response.tool_call?
314: 
315:       response.content = JSON.parse(response.content)
316:     rescue JSON::ParserError
317:       # If parsing fails, keep content as string.
318:     end
319: 
320:     def set_legacy_callback(name, legacy_name, additive_name, &block)
321:       warn_legacy_callback_deprecation(legacy_name, additive_name) if block
322: 
323:       @on[name] = block
324:       self
325:     end
326: 
327:     def warn_legacy_callback_deprecation(legacy_name, additive_name)
328:       RubyLLM.deprecator.warn(
329:         "`#{legacy_name}` is deprecated and will be removed in RubyLLM 2.0. " \
330:         "Use `#{additive_name}` instead."
331:       )
332:     end
333: 
334:     def run_callbacks(name, legacy_name, *args)
335:       @callbacks[name].each { |callback| callback.call(*args) }
336:       @on[legacy_name]&.call(*args)
337:     end
338: 
339:     def wrap_streaming_block(&block)
340:       return nil unless block
341: 
342:       run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
343: 
344:       block
345:     end
346: 
347:     def handle_tool_calls(response, &)
348:       halt_result = if concurrency
349:                       handle_concurrent_tool_calls(response.tool_calls)
350:                     else
351:                       handle_sequential_tool_calls(response.tool_calls)
352:                     end
353: 
354:       reset_tool_choice if forced_tool_choice?
355:       halt_result || complete(&)
356:     end
357: 
358:     def handle_sequential_tool_calls(tool_calls)
359:       halt_result = nil
360: 
361:       tool_calls.each_value do |tool_call|
362:         run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
363:         result = execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
364:         add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
365:         halt_result = result if result.is_a?(Tool::Halt)
366:       end
367: 
368:       halt_result
369:     end
370: 
371:     def handle_concurrent_tool_calls(tool_calls)
372:       halt_result = nil
373: 
374:       execute_tools_concurrently(tool_calls) do |tool_call, result|
375:         run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
376:         add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
377:         halt_result = result if result.is_a?(Tool::Halt)
378:       end
379: 
380:       halt_result
381:     end
382: 
383:     def execute_tools_concurrently(tool_calls, &on_result)
384:       ToolConcurrency.run(concurrency, tool_calls, on_result:) do |tool_call|
385:         execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
386:       end
387:     end
388: 
389:     def execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
390:       run_callbacks(:before_tool_call, :tool_call, tool_call)
391:       result = execute_tool tool_call
392:       run_callbacks(:after_tool_result, :tool_result, result)
393:       result
394:     end
395: 
396:     def add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
397:       tool_payload = result.is_a?(Tool::Halt) ? result.content : result
398:       content = content_like?(tool_payload) ? tool_payload : tool_payload.to_s
399:       message = add_message role: :tool, content:, tool_call_id: tool_call.id
400:       run_callbacks(:after_message, :end_message, message)
401:       message
402:     end
403: 
404:     def execute_tool(tool_call)
405:       tool = tools[tool_call.name.to_sym]
406:       if tool.nil?
407:         return {
408:           error: "Model tried to call unavailable tool `#{tool_call.name}`. " \
409:                  "Available tools: #{tools.keys.to_json}."
410:         }
411:       end
412: 
413:       args = tool_call.arguments
414:       payload = {
415:         chat: self,
416:         provider: @provider.slug,
417:         provider_class: @provider.class.name,
418:         model: @model.id,
419:         model_info: @model,
420:         tool: tool,
421:         tool_call: tool_call,
422:         tool_name: tool.name,
423:         tool_arguments: args,
424:         tool_call_id: tool_call.id
425:       }
426: 
427:       RubyLLM.instrument('tool_call.ruby_llm', payload, config: @config) do |event|
428:         result = tool.call(args)
429:         event[:result] = result
430:         event[:result_content] = result.is_a?(Tool::Halt) ? result.content : result
431:         event[:result_class] = result.class.name
432:         result
433:       end
434:     end
435: 
436:     def update_tool_options(choice:, calls:)
437:       unless choice.nil?
438:         normalized_choice = normalize_tool_choice(choice)
439:         valid_tool_choices = %i[auto none required] + tools.keys
440:         unless valid_tool_choices.include?(normalized_choice)
441:           raise InvalidToolChoiceError,
442:                 "Invalid tool choice: #{choice}. Valid choices are: #{valid_tool_choices.join(', ')}"
443:         end
444: 
445:         @tool_prefs[:choice] = normalized_choice
446:       end
447: 
448:       @tool_prefs[:calls] = normalize_calls(calls) unless calls.nil?
449:     end
450: 
451:     def update_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
452:       @concurrency = normalize_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
453:     end
454: 
455:     def normalize_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
456:       return nil if concurrency.nil? || concurrency == false
457:       return :threads if concurrency == true
458: 
459:       normalized = concurrency.to_sym
460:       return normalized if ToolConcurrency::MODES.include?(normalized)
461: 
462:       raise ArgumentError,
463:             "Unknown tool concurrency: #{concurrency.inspect}. " \
464:             "Available modes: #{ToolConcurrency::MODES.join(', ')}"
465:     end
466: 
467:     def normalize_calls(calls)
468:       case calls
469:       when :many, 'many'
470:         :many
471:       when :one, 'one', 1
472:         :one
473:       else
474:         raise ArgumentError, "Invalid calls value: #{calls.inspect}. Valid values are: :many, :one, or 1"
475:       end
476:     end
477: 
478:     def normalize_tool_choice(choice)
479:       return choice.to_sym if choice.is_a?(String) || choice.is_a?(Symbol)
480:       return tool_name_for_choice_class(choice) if choice.is_a?(Class)
481: 
482:       choice.respond_to?(:name) ? choice.name.to_sym : choice.to_sym
483:     end
484: 
485:     def tool_name_for_choice_class(tool_class)
486:       matched_tool_name = tools.find { |_name, tool| tool.is_a?(tool_class) }&.first
487:       return matched_tool_name if matched_tool_name
488: 
489:       classify_tool_name(tool_class.name)
490:     end
491: 
492:     def classify_tool_name(class_name)
493:       Utils.underscore(class_name.split('::').last).delete_suffix('_tool').to_sym
494:     end
495: 
496:     def forced_tool_choice?
497:       @tool_prefs[:choice] && !%i[auto none].include?(@tool_prefs[:choice])
498:     end
499: 
500:     def reset_tool_choice
501:       @tool_prefs[:choice] = nil
502:     end
503: 
504:     def build_content(message, attachments)
505:       return message if content_like?(message)
506: 
507:       Content.new(message, attachments)
508:     end
509: 
510:     def content_like?(object)
511:       object.is_a?(Content) || object.is_a?(Content::Raw)
512:     end
513: 
514:     def append_system_instruction(instructions)
515:       system_messages, non_system_messages = @messages.partition { |msg| msg.role == :system }
516:       system_messages << Message.new(role: :system, content: instructions)
517:       @messages = system_messages + non_system_messages
518:     end
519: 
520:     def replace_system_instruction(instructions)
521:       system_messages, non_system_messages = @messages.partition { |msg| msg.role == :system }
522: 
523:       if system_messages.empty?
524:         system_messages = [Message.new(role: :system, content: instructions)]
525:       else
526:         system_messages.first.content = instructions
527:         system_messages = [system_messages.first]
528:       end
529: 
530:       @messages = system_messages + non_system_messages
531:     end
532:   end
533: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Chat methods of the OpenAI API integration
7:       module Chat
8:         def completion_url
9:           'chat/completions'
10:         end
11: 
12:         module_function
13: 
14:         # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists,Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
15:         def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false, schema: nil,
16:                            thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
17:           warn_unsupported_citations(model) if citations && !model.citations?
18:           tool_prefs ||= {}
19:           payload = {
20:             model: model.id,
21:             messages: format_messages(messages),
22:             stream: stream
23:           }
24: 
25:           payload[:temperature] = temperature unless temperature.nil?
26:           if tools.any?
27:             payload[:tools] = tools.map { |_, tool| tool_for(tool) }
28:             payload[:tool_choice] = build_tool_choice(tool_prefs[:choice]) unless tool_prefs[:choice].nil?
29:             payload[:parallel_tool_calls] = tool_prefs[:calls] == :many unless tool_prefs[:calls].nil?
30:           end
31: 
32:           if schema
33:             schema_name = schema[:name]
34:             schema_def = schema[:schema]
35:             strict = schema[:strict]
36: 
37:             payload[:response_format] = {
38:               type: 'json_schema',
39:               json_schema: {
40:                 name: schema_name,
41:                 schema: schema_def,
42:                 strict: strict
43:               }
44:             }
45:           end
46: 
47:           effort = resolve_effort(thinking)
48:           payload[:reasoning_effort] = effort if effort
49: 
50:           payload[:stream_options] = { include_usage: true } if stream
51:           payload
52:         end
53:         # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists,Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
54: 
55:         def warn_unsupported_citations(model)
56:           RubyLLM.logger.warn(
57:             "#{model.id} does not support citations according to the model registry. " \
58:             'with_citations may have no effect.'
59:           )
60:         end
61: 
62:         def parse_completion_response(response)
63:           data = response.body
64:           return if data.nil? || data.empty?
65: 
66:           raise Error.new(response, data.dig('error', 'message')) if data.dig('error', 'message')
67: 
68:           message_data = data.dig('choices', 0, 'message')
69:           return unless message_data
70: 
71:           usage = data['usage'] || {}
72:           thinking_tokens = thinking_tokens(usage)
73:           content, thinking_from_blocks = extract_content_and_thinking(message_data['content'])
74:           thinking_text = thinking_from_blocks || extract_thinking_text(message_data)
75:           thinking_signature = extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
76: 
77:           Message.new(
78:             role: :assistant,
79:             content: content,
80:             citations: extract_citations(message_data, data, content),
81:             thinking: Thinking.build(text: thinking_text, signature: thinking_signature),
82:             tool_calls: parse_tool_calls(message_data['tool_calls']),
83:             input_tokens: input_tokens(usage),
84:             output_tokens: output_tokens(usage),
85:             cached_tokens: cache_read_tokens(usage),
86:             cache_creation_tokens: cache_write_tokens(usage),
87:             thinking_tokens: thinking_tokens,
88:             model_id: data['model'],
89:             raw: response
90:           )
91:         end
92: 
93:         def input_tokens(usage)
94:           return usage['prompt_cache_miss_tokens'] if usage['prompt_cache_miss_tokens']
95: 
96:           prompt_tokens = usage['prompt_tokens']
97:           return unless prompt_tokens
98: 
99:           [prompt_tokens.to_i - cache_read_tokens(usage).to_i - cache_write_tokens(usage).to_i, 0].max
100:         end
101: 
102:         def output_tokens(usage)
103:           completion_tokens = usage['completion_tokens']
104:           return unless completion_tokens
105: 
106:           completion_tokens = completion_tokens.to_i
107:           generated_tokens = generated_tokens_from_total(usage)
108:           return completion_tokens unless generated_tokens && generated_tokens > completion_tokens
109: 
110:           generated_tokens
111:         end
112: 
113:         def generated_tokens_from_total(usage)
114:           prompt_tokens = usage['prompt_tokens']
115:           total_tokens = usage['total_tokens']
116:           return unless prompt_tokens && total_tokens
117: 
118:           [total_tokens.to_i - prompt_tokens.to_i, 0].max
119:         end
120: 
121:         def cache_read_tokens(usage)
122:           usage.dig('prompt_tokens_details', 'cached_tokens') || usage['prompt_cache_hit_tokens']
123:         end
124: 
125:         def cache_write_tokens(usage)
126:           usage.dig('prompt_tokens_details', 'cache_write_tokens') || 0
127:         end
128: 
129:         def thinking_tokens(usage)
130:           usage.dig('completion_tokens_details', 'reasoning_tokens') || usage['reasoning_tokens']
131:         end
132: 
133:         def extract_citations(message_data, data, content)
134:           annotations = parse_annotations(message_data['annotations'], content)
135:           return annotations if annotations.any?
136: 
137:           parse_root_citations(data)
138:         end
139: 
140:         def parse_annotations(annotations, content)
141:           Array(annotations).filter_map do |annotation|
142:             details = annotation['url_citation']
143:             next unless details.is_a?(Hash)
144: 
145:             start_index = details['start_index']
146:             end_index = details['end_index']
147: 
148:             Citation.new(
149:               url: details['url'],
150:               title: details['title'],
151:               text: annotated_text(content, start_index, end_index),
152:               start_index: start_index,
153:               end_index: end_index
154:             )
155:           end
156:         end
157: 
158:         def annotated_text(content, start_index, end_index)
159:           return nil unless content.is_a?(String) && start_index && end_index
160: 
161:           content[start_index...end_index]
162:         end
163: 
164:         # Perplexity and xAI return search citations at the root of the response.
165:         def parse_root_citations(data)
166:           search_results = data['search_results']
167:           return parse_search_results(search_results) if search_results.is_a?(Array) && search_results.any?
168: 
169:           Array(data['citations']).each_with_index.filter_map do |url, index|
170:             Citation.new(url: url, source_index: index) if url.is_a?(String)
171:           end
172:         end
173: 
174:         def parse_search_results(results)
175:           results.each_with_index.filter_map do |result, index|
176:             next unless result.is_a?(Hash)
177: 
178:             Citation.new(
179:               url: result['url'],
180:               title: result['title'],
181:               cited_text: result['snippet'],
182:               source_index: index
183:             )
184:           end
185:         end
186: 
187:         def format_messages(messages)
188:           messages.map do |msg|
189:             {
190:               role: format_role(msg.role),
191:               content: format_message_content(msg),
192:               tool_calls: format_tool_calls(msg.tool_calls),
193:               tool_call_id: msg.tool_call_id
194:             }.compact.merge(format_thinking(msg))
195:           end
196:         end
197: 
198:         def format_message_content(msg)
199:           content = format_content(msg.content)
200:           return '' if content.nil? && thinking_only_assistant_message?(msg)
201: 
202:           content
203:         end
204: 
205:         def thinking_only_assistant_message?(msg)
206:           msg.role == :assistant && msg.thinking && !msg.tool_call?
207:         end
208: 
209:         def format_content(content)
210:           Media.format_content(content)
211:         end
212: 
213:         def format_role(role)
214:           case role
215:           when :system
216:             @config.openai_use_system_role ? 'system' : 'developer'
217:           else
218:             role.to_s
219:           end
220:         end
221: 
222:         def resolve_effort(thinking)
223:           return nil unless thinking
224: 
225:           thinking.respond_to?(:effort) ? thinking.effort : thinking
226:         end
227: 
228:         def format_thinking(msg)
229:           return {} unless msg.role == :assistant
230: 
231:           thinking = msg.thinking
232:           return {} unless thinking
233: 
234:           payload = {}
235:           if thinking.text
236:             payload[:reasoning] = thinking.text
237:             payload[:reasoning_content] = thinking.text
238:           end
239:           payload[:reasoning_signature] = thinking.signature if thinking.signature
240:           payload
241:         end
242: 
243:         def extract_thinking_text(message_data)
244:           candidate = message_data['reasoning_content'] || message_data['reasoning'] || message_data['thinking']
245:           candidate.is_a?(String) ? candidate : nil
246:         end
247: 
248:         def extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
249:           candidate = message_data['reasoning_signature'] || message_data['signature']
250:           candidate.is_a?(String) ? candidate : nil
251:         end
252: 
253:         def extract_content_and_thinking(content)
254:           return extract_think_tag_content(content) if content.is_a?(String)
255:           return [content, nil] unless content.is_a?(Array)
256: 
257:           text = extract_text_from_blocks(content)
258:           thinking = extract_thinking_from_blocks(content)
259: 
260:           [text.empty? ? nil : text, thinking.empty? ? nil : thinking]
261:         end
262: 
263:         def extract_text_from_blocks(blocks)
264:           blocks.filter_map do |block|
265:             block['text'] if block['type'] == 'text' && block['text'].is_a?(String)
266:           end.join
267:         end
268: 
269:         def extract_thinking_from_blocks(blocks)
270:           blocks.filter_map do |block|
271:             next unless block['type'] == 'thinking'
272: 
273:             extract_thinking_text_from_block(block)
274:           end.join
275:         end
276: 
277:         def extract_thinking_text_from_block(block)
278:           thinking_block = block['thinking']
279:           return thinking_block if thinking_block.is_a?(String)
280: 
281:           if thinking_block.is_a?(Array)
282:             return thinking_block.filter_map { |item| item['text'] if item['type'] == 'text' }.join
283:           end
284: 
285:           block['text'] if block['text'].is_a?(String)
286:         end
287: 
288:         def extract_think_tag_content(text)
289:           return [text, nil] unless text.include?('<think>')
290: 
291:           thinking = text.scan(%r{<think>(.*?)</think>}m).join
292:           content = text.gsub(%r{<think>.*?</think>}m, '').strip
293: 
294:           [content.empty? ? nil : content, thinking.empty? ? nil : thinking]
295:         end
296:       end
297:     end
298:   end
299: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class Anthropic
6:       # Chat methods for the Anthropic API implementation
7:       module Chat
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def completion_url
11:           'v1/messages'
12:         end
13: 
14:         # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
15:         def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false,
16:                            schema: nil, thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
17:           warn_unsupported_citations(model) if citations && !model.citations?
18:           tool_prefs ||= {}
19:           system_messages, chat_messages = separate_messages(messages)
20:           system_content = build_system_content(system_messages)
21: 
22:           build_base_payload(chat_messages, model, stream, thinking, citations: citations).tap do |payload|
23:             add_optional_fields(payload, system_content:, tools:, tool_prefs:, temperature:, schema:)
24:           end
25:         end
26:         # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
27: 
28:         def warn_unsupported_citations(model)
29:           RubyLLM.logger.warn(
30:             "#{model.id} does not support citations according to the model registry. " \
31:             'with_citations may have no effect.'
32:           )
33:         end
34: 
35:         def separate_messages(messages)
36:           messages.partition { |msg| msg.role == :system }
37:         end
38: 
39:         def build_system_content(system_messages)
40:           return [] if system_messages.empty?
41: 
42:           # Anthropic's `system` parameter accepts an array of text content blocks
43:           # (each optionally with cache_control); each :system message becomes its
44:           # own block in the resulting array.
45:           system_messages.flat_map do |msg|
46:             content = msg.content
47: 
48:             if content.is_a?(RubyLLM::Content::Raw)
49:               content.value
50:             else
51:               Media.format_content(content)
52:             end
53:           end
54:         end
55: 
56:         def build_base_payload(chat_messages, model, stream, thinking, citations: false)
57:           payload = {
58:             model: model.id,
59:             messages: chat_messages.map { |msg| format_message(msg, thinking: thinking, citations: citations) },
60:             stream: stream,
61:             max_tokens: model.max_tokens || 4096
62:           }
63: 
64:           add_thinking_fields(payload, thinking, model)
65: 
66:           payload
67:         end
68: 
69:         def add_optional_fields(payload, system_content:, tools:, tool_prefs:, temperature:, schema: nil) # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
70:           if tools.any?
71:             payload[:tools] = tools.values.map { |t| Tools.function_for(t) }
72:             unless tool_prefs[:choice].nil? && tool_prefs[:calls].nil?
73:               payload[:tool_choice] = Tools.build_tool_choice(tool_prefs)
74:             end
75:           end
76:           payload[:system] = system_content unless system_content.empty?
77:           payload[:temperature] = temperature unless temperature.nil?
78:           payload[:output_config] = payload.fetch(:output_config, {}).merge(build_output_config(schema)) if schema
79:         end
80: 
81:         def build_output_config(schema)
82:           normalized = RubyLLM::Utils.deep_dup(schema[:schema])
83:           normalized.delete(:strict)
84:           normalized.delete('strict')
85:           { format: { type: 'json_schema', schema: normalized } }
86:         end
87: 
88:         def parse_completion_response(response)
89:           data = response.body
90:           content_blocks = data['content'] || []
91: 
92:           text_content, citations = extract_text_and_citations(content_blocks)
93:           thinking_content = extract_thinking_content(content_blocks)
94:           thinking_signature = extract_thinking_signature(content_blocks)
95:           tool_use_blocks = Tools.find_tool_uses(content_blocks)
96: 
97:           build_message(data, text_content, citations, thinking_content, thinking_signature, tool_use_blocks,
98:                         response)
99:         end
100: 
101:         def extract_text_and_citations(blocks)
102:           text = +''
103:           citations = []
104: 
105:           blocks.each do |block|
106:             next unless block['type'] == 'text'
107: 
108:             block_text = block['text'].to_s
109:             Array(block['citations']).each do |citation|
110:               citations << parse_citation(citation, text: block_text,
111:                                                     start_index: text.length,
112:                                                     end_index: text.length + block_text.length)
113:             end
114:             text << block_text
115:           end
116: 
117:           [text, citations]
118:         end
119: 
120:         def parse_citation(data, text: nil, start_index: nil, end_index: nil)
121:           end_page = data['end_page_number']
122: 
123:           Citation.new(
124:             url: citation_url(data),
125:             title: data['document_title'] || data['title'],
126:             cited_text: data['cited_text'],
127:             text: text,
128:             start_index: start_index,
129:             end_index: end_index,
130:             source_index: data['document_index'] || data['search_result_index'],
131:             start_page: data['start_page_number'],
132:             end_page: end_page && (end_page - 1)
133:           )
134:         end
135: 
136:         # Search result citations carry the developer-provided source string.
137:         def citation_url(data)
138:           url = data['url'] || data['source']
139:           url if url&.match?(%r{\Ahttps?://})
140:         end
141: 
142:         def extract_thinking_content(blocks)
143:           thinking_blocks = blocks.select { |c| c['type'] == 'thinking' }
144:           thoughts = thinking_blocks.map { |c| c['thinking'] || c['text'] }.join
145:           thoughts.empty? ? nil : thoughts
146:         end
147: 
148:         def extract_thinking_signature(blocks)
149:           thinking_block = blocks.find { |c| c['type'] == 'thinking' } ||
150:                            blocks.find { |c| c['type'] == 'redacted_thinking' }
151:           thinking_block&.dig('signature') || thinking_block&.dig('data')
152:         end
153: 
154:         def build_message(data, content, citations, thinking, thinking_signature, tool_use_blocks, response) # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
155:           usage = data['usage'] || {}
156:           thinking_tokens = usage.dig('output_tokens_details', 'thinking_tokens') ||
157:                             usage.dig('output_tokens_details', 'reasoning_tokens') ||
158:                             usage['thinking_tokens'] ||
159:                             usage['reasoning_tokens']
160: 
161:           Message.new(
162:             role: :assistant,
163:             content: content,
164:             citations: citations,
165:             thinking: Thinking.build(text: thinking, signature: thinking_signature),
166:             tool_calls: Tools.parse_tool_calls(tool_use_blocks),
167:             input_tokens: usage['input_tokens'],
168:             output_tokens: usage['output_tokens'],
169:             cached_tokens: extract_cached_tokens(data),
170:             cache_creation_tokens: extract_cache_creation_tokens(data),
171:             thinking_tokens: thinking_tokens,
172:             model_id: data['model'],
173:             raw: response
174:           )
175:         end
176: 
177:         def format_message(msg, thinking: nil, citations: false)
178:           thinking_enabled = thinking&.enabled?
179: 
180:           if msg.tool_call?
181:             format_tool_call_with_thinking(msg, thinking_enabled)
182:           elsif msg.tool_result?
183:             Tools.format_tool_result(msg)
184:           else
185:             format_basic_message_with_thinking(msg, thinking_enabled, citations: citations)
186:           end
187:         end
188: 
189:         def format_basic_message_with_thinking(msg, thinking_enabled, citations: false)
190:           content_blocks = []
191: 
192:           if msg.role == :assistant && thinking_enabled
193:             thinking_block = build_thinking_block(msg.thinking)
194:             content_blocks << thinking_block if thinking_block
195:           end
196: 
197:           append_formatted_content(content_blocks, msg.content, citations: citations)
198: 
199:           {
200:             role: convert_role(msg.role),
201:             content: content_blocks
202:           }
203:         end
204: 
205:         def format_tool_call_with_thinking(msg, thinking_enabled)
206:           if msg.content.is_a?(RubyLLM::Content::Raw)
207:             content_blocks = msg.content.value
208:             content_blocks = [content_blocks] unless content_blocks.is_a?(Array)
209:             content_blocks = prepend_thinking_block(content_blocks, msg, thinking_enabled)
210: 
211:             return { role: 'assistant', content: content_blocks }
212:           end
213: 
214:           content_blocks = prepend_thinking_block([], msg, thinking_enabled)
215:           append_formatted_content(content_blocks, msg.content) unless msg.content.nil? || msg.content.empty?
216: 
217:           msg.tool_calls.each_value do |tool_call|
218:             content_blocks << {
219:               type: 'tool_use',
220:               id: tool_call.id,
221:               name: tool_call.name,
222:               input: tool_call.arguments
223:             }
224:           end
225: 
226:           {
227:             role: 'assistant',
228:             content: content_blocks
229:           }
230:         end
231: 
232:         def prepend_thinking_block(content_blocks, msg, thinking_enabled)
233:           return content_blocks unless thinking_enabled
234: 
235:           thinking_block = build_thinking_block(msg.thinking)
236:           content_blocks.unshift(thinking_block) if thinking_block
237: 
238:           content_blocks
239:         end
240: 
241:         def build_thinking_block(thinking)
242:           return nil unless thinking
243: 
244:           if thinking.text
245:             {
246:               type: 'thinking',
247:               thinking: thinking.text,
248:               signature: thinking.signature
249:             }.compact
250:           elsif thinking.signature
251:             {
252:               type: 'redacted_thinking',
253:               data: thinking.signature
254:             }
255:           end
256:         end
257: 
258:         def append_formatted_content(content_blocks, content, citations: false)
259:           formatted_content = Media.format_content(content, citations: citations)
260:           if formatted_content.is_a?(Array)
261:             content_blocks.concat(formatted_content)
262:           else
263:             content_blocks << formatted_content
264:           end
265:         end
266: 
267:         def convert_role(role)
268:           case role
269:           when :tool, :user then 'user'
270:           else 'assistant'
271:           end
272:         end
273: 
274:         def add_thinking_fields(payload, thinking, model)
275:           thinking_payload = build_thinking_payload(thinking, model)
276:           return unless thinking_payload
277: 
278:           payload[:thinking] = thinking_payload[:thinking] if thinking_payload[:thinking]
279:           return unless thinking_payload[:output_config]
280: 
281:           payload[:output_config] = payload.fetch(:output_config, {}).merge(thinking_payload[:output_config])
282:         end
283: 
284:         def build_thinking_payload(thinking, model)
285:           return nil unless thinking&.enabled?
286: 
287:           effort = resolve_effort(thinking)
288:           return nil if effort == 'none'
289: 
290:           budget = resolve_budget(thinking)
291:           if budget
292:             return enabled_thinking_payload(budget) if model.reasoning_option('budget_tokens')
293: 
294:             raise ArgumentError, "Anthropic thinking budget is not supported for #{model.id}"
295:           end
296: 
297:           raise ArgumentError, 'Anthropic adaptive thinking requires an effort' if effort.nil?
298:           return adaptive_thinking_payload(effort) if model.reasoning_option('effort')
299: 
300:           raise ArgumentError, "Anthropic thinking effort is not supported for #{model.id}"
301:         end
302: 
303:         def enabled_thinking_payload(budget)
304:           {
305:             thinking: {
306:               type: 'enabled',
307:               budget_tokens: budget
308:             }
309:           }
310:         end
311: 
312:         def adaptive_thinking_payload(effort)
313:           {
314:             thinking: { type: 'adaptive' },
315:             output_config: { effort: effort }
316:           }
317:         end
318: 
319:         def resolve_effort(thinking)
320:           effort = thinking.effort.to_s
321:           effort.empty? ? nil : effort
322:         end
323: 
324:         def resolve_budget(thinking)
325:           thinking.budget
326:         end
327:       end
328:     end
329:   end
330: end

351: def generate_aliases # rubocop:disable Metrics/AbcSize, Metrics/CyclomaticComplexity, Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
352:   models = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }

354:   RubyLLM.models.all.each do |model|

358:   aliases = {}

360:   # OpenAI models
361:   models['openai'].each do |model|
362:     openrouter_model = "openai/#{model}"
363:     azure_model = models['azure'].include?(model) ? model : nil
364:     next unless models['openrouter'].include?(openrouter_model)
365: 
366:     alias_key = model.gsub('-latest', '')
367:     aliases[alias_key] = {
368:       'openai' => model,
369:       'openrouter' => openrouter_model
370:     }
371:     aliases[alias_key]['azure'] = azure_model if azure_model
372:   end

374:   anthropic_latest = group_anthropic_models_by_base_name(models['anthropic'])
375: 
376:   anthropic_latest.each do |base_name, latest_model|
377:     openrouter_variants = [
378:       "anthropic/#{base_name}",
379:       "anthropic/#{base_name.gsub(/-(\d)/, '.\1')}",
380:       "anthropic/#{base_name.gsub(/claude-(\d+)-(\d+)/, 'claude-\1.\2')}",

384:     openrouter_model = openrouter_variants.find { |v| models['openrouter'].include?(v) }

391:     aliases[base_name]['bedrock'] = bedrock_model if bedrock_model
392:     aliases[base_name]['azure'] = latest_model if models['azure'].include?(latest_model)

396:     next unless bedrock_model.start_with?('anthropic.')

413:     aliases[anthropic_name]['openrouter'] = openrouter_model if openrouter_model

504: def group_anthropic_models_by_base_name(anthropic_models)
505:   grouped = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
506: 
507:   anthropic_models.each do |model|
508:     base_name = extract_base_name(model)
509:     grouped[base_name] << model
510:   end
511: 
512:   latest_models = {}
513:   grouped.each do |base_name, model_list|
514:     if model_list.size == 1
515:       latest_models[base_name] = model_list.first
516:     else
517:       latest_model = model_list.max_by { |model| extract_date_from_model(model) }
518:       latest_models[base_name] = latest_model
519:     end
520:   end
521: 
522:   latest_models
523: end

525: def extract_base_name(model)
526:   if model =~ /^(.+)-(\d{8})$/
527:     Regexp.last_match(1)
528:   else
529:     model
530:   end
531: end
532: 
533: def extract_date_from_model(model)
534:   if model =~ /-(\d{8})$/
535:     Regexp.last_match(1)
536:   else
537:     '00000000'
538:   end
539: end

541: def find_best_bedrock_model(anthropic_model, bedrock_models) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
542:   base_pattern = case anthropic_model
543:                  when 'claude-2.0', 'claude-2'
544:                    'claude-v2'
545:                  when 'claude-2.1'
546:                    'claude-v2:1'
547:                  when 'claude-instant-v1', 'claude-instant'
548:                    'claude-instant'
549:                  else
550:                    anthropic_model
551:                  end
552: 
553:   matching_models = bedrock_models.select do |bedrock_model|
554:     model_without_prefix = bedrock_model.sub(/^(?:(?:[a-z]{2}|global)\.)?anthropic\./, '')
555:     model_without_prefix.match?(/\A#{Regexp.escape(base_pattern)}(?:-v\d+|:\d+k|$)/)
556:   end
557: 
558:   return nil if matching_models.empty?
559: 
560:   begin
561:     model_info = RubyLLM.models.find(anthropic_model)
562:     target_context = model_info.context_window
563:   rescue StandardError
564:     target_context = nil
565:   end
566: 
567:   if target_context
568:     target_k = target_context / 1000
569: 
570:     with_context = matching_models.select do |m|
571:       m.include?(":#{target_k}k") || m.include?(":0:#{target_k}k")
572:     end
573: 
574:     return with_context.first if with_context.any?
575:   end
576: 
577:   matching_models.min_by do |model|
578:     context_priority = if model =~ /:(?:\d+:)?(\d+)k/
579:                          -Regexp.last_match(1).to_i
580:                        else
581:                          0
582:                        end
583: 
584:     version_priority = if model =~ /-v(\d+):/
585:                          -Regexp.last_match(1).to_i
586:                        else
587:                          0
588:                        end
589: 
590:     has_context_priority = model.include?('k') ? -1 : 0
591:     [has_context_priority, context_priority, version_priority]
592:   end
593: end
